name: slack-gif-creator
description: Toolkit for creating animated GIFs optimized for Slack, with validators for size constraints and composable animation primitives. This skill applies when users request animated GIFs or emoji animations for Slack from descriptions like "make me a GIF for Slack of X doing Y".
capabilities: Creates GIFs optimized for Slack 64KB emoji and 2MB message limits
Provides 15+ animation primitives including bounce, spin, pulse, explode
Includes professional easing functions for natural motion
Validates GIFs against Slack size and dimension constraints
Supports text rendering with outlines for small GIFs
author: DYAI2025
securitySummary: This is a legitimate GIF creation toolkit with no security concerns. The static scanner produced false positives: variable name 'c2' in easing formulas was flagged as C2 keyword, shell command examples in documentation were flagged as execution risks, and standard font path access was flagged as reconnaissance. No actual cryptographic algorithms, network calls, or command injection vulnerabilities exist. This skill only creates local image files using standard Python imaging libraries.
valueStatement: Slack has strict size limits for GIFs (64KB for emoji, 2MB for messages). This toolkit creates optimized animations that meet these constraints while providing professional animation effects.
userTitle: Create custom animated GIFs for Slack messages and emoji
limitations: Requires Python with PIL, imageio, and numpy dependencies
Emoji GIFs limited to 64KB with strict size constraints
Cannot convert existing video files or images to GIFs
Font rendering varies across different operating systems

useCases.title: Create custom emoji reactions
useCases.description: Design animated emoji that match your team culture and inside jokes, optimized for Slack 64KB limit.

useCases.title: Make engaging announcement GIFs
useCases.description: Create professional animated messages for product launches, celebrations, or important updates.

useCases.title: Build team morale with fun animations
useCases.description: Generate celebratory GIFs for team achievements, birthdays, or milestone celebrations.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Emoji Animation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a pulsing heart emoji that grows and shrinks smoothly, optimized for Slack emoji at 128x128px

promptTemplates.title: Celebration Animation
promptTemplates.prompt: Make a GIF showing confetti falling with TEAM WIN text appearing, 480x480px for Slack message

promptTemplates.title: Loading Spinner
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an animated loading spinner using rotating dots, professional blue color scheme, emoji size

promptTemplates.title: Reaction Sequence
promptTemplates.prompt: Make a GIF that morphs from neutral to surprised to amazed emoji with impact effects

bestPractices: Always validate GIF size after creation, especially for emoji (64KB limit is strict)
Use fewer frames and solid colors for emoji GIFs - avoid gradients to reduce file size
Add text outlines for readability on small GIFs and different backgrounds
antiPatterns: Do not use more than 15 frames for emoji GIFs - exceeds size limits
Avoid complex gradients in emoji animations - they increase file size significantly
Never skip size validation - upload failures waste time and frustrate users

faq.question: Why do my emoji GIFs fail to upload to Slack?
faq.answer: Slack enforces a strict 64KB limit for emoji. Use validators to check size and reduce frames or colors.

faq.question: Can I use existing images or videos to make GIFs?
faq.answer: This toolkit creates animations programmatically. It generates new animations from code, not media conversion.

faq.question: How do I ensure my GIFs look good on all devices?
faq.answer: Use professional color palettes and add text outlines for readability across different Slack themes.

faq.question: Is this safe to use on company computers?
faq.answer: Yes, the toolkit creates image files locally with no network access beyond standard Python libraries.

faq.question: My GIF is over the size limit. How do I fix it?
faq.answer: Reduce frame count, lower color depth, simplify animations, or decrease dimensions. Suggestions provided.

faq.question: How does this compare to online GIF makers?
faq.answer: This toolkit gives precise control over Slack requirements and creates files that meet exact constraints.
